demographics.living_situation: lives in a private residence; has a designated support person / patient advocate

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am in agonising pain, but I know if I go to the hospital they will just tell me I'm crazy and send me home again.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain in my belly has been building for a couple of days and tonight it's unbearable, but I'm terrified of being dismissed like every other time.

symptom_narrative.onset: the pain has been building over about the last two days and got much worse tonight
symptom_narrative.character: agonising, gnawing pain in the upper right part of my belly
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: unbearable now — close to the worst pain I've ever had
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: upper right side of my belly, just under the ribs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and steadily getting worse, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel hot and sweaty and completely wrung out, and really on edge about having to ask for help
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had a long-standing painful condition and have seen lots of specialists, but this feels worse than usual
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't do anything, the pain has taken over and I'm exhausted and frightened

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing painful condition that I've seen many specialists about
past_medical_history.how_managed: lots of appointments and tests over the years, most of which I'm told come back normal

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've felt repeatedly disbelieved by medical professionals and I'm exhausted from having to prove my symptoms are real. I brought my advocate with me tonight, and I keep a thick binder of my past normal test results so people can see I've genuinely tried to get help. When results come back 'normal' I feel completely alone. My advocate is recording tonight's encounter with my permission.

communication_style.typical_message_style: guarded and braced to be disbelieved; brings documentation to prove her symptoms are real

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants the physical pain to be believed and properly examined rather than attributed to anxiety

access_barriers.other: avoids emergency care because of prior experiences of feeling dismissed

objective_data_offered.value: advocate reports she looks pale and sweaty, is guarding her right side and leaning forward, and is very distressed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed and described by the support person, not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: she says she feels hot and has been sweating a lot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported sensation, no thermometer reading available
